name: Driving Licence
dataSource: parivahan.gov.in — Ministry of Road Transport & Highways, Government of India

sections.purpose: A Driving Licence (DL) is an official document issued by the Transport Department (via RTO) that legally authorises a person to drive motor vehicles on public roads in India. As per the Motor Vehicles Act, no person shall drive a motor vehicle in any public place unless they hold an effective driving licence. It is also widely accepted as photo identity and address proof. Source: parivahan.gov.in
sections.eligibility: Age requirements (as per Motor Vehicles Act): 18 years or above for motorcycles with gear, Light Motor Vehicles (cars, jeeps), and transport vehicles (with additional conditions). 16 years or above for gearless two-wheelers (motorcycles without gear, scooters up to 50cc engine) — with written consent of parent/guardian. 20 years or above for transport/commercial vehicles. Medical fitness: Applicant must be physically and mentally fit. Medical Certificate in Form 1 (for all) and Form 1A (for applicants aged 40 and above or for transport vehicles) required. Must not be disqualified by any court from holding a licence. Source: parivahan.gov.in
documents: Aadhaar card (serves as identity, address, and age proof — accepted at most RTOs)
Application Form 1 — Medical Certificate (self-declaration for fitness)
Application Form 1A — Medical Certificate from a registered medical practitioner (mandatory for age 40+ and for transport vehicles). Source: parivahan.gov.in
Existing valid Learner Licence (mandatory when applying for permanent DL — must have completed minimum 30 days after LL issue date)
Passport-size photographs (as required by the RTO)
Age proof if not on Aadhaar: Birth Certificate or SSLC/10th Certificate
For the driving test: Vehicle used must have valid Registration Certificate (RC) and valid Insurance
sections.fees: Fees as per Central Motor Vehicles Rules (parivahan.gov.in): Learner Licence (LL) for each class of vehicle: ₹200. Permanent Driving Licence (DL): ₹200. Renewal of DL: ₹200. Additional endorsement for new vehicle class: ₹500. International Driving Permit (IDP): ₹1,000. Duplicate DL (lost/damaged): ₹200. Late renewal fee (after 1 year of expiry): ₹300 + ₹1,000 per year of delay. Driving test fee (if failed and retesting): additional fee — check state-specific rates at sarathi.parivahan.gov.in. Source: parivahan.gov.in
sections.processingTime: Learner Licence: issued within 1–7 days after passing the LL test (some RTOs issue same day). Minimum 30 days must pass after LL issue before applying for permanent DL. Permanent DL: 15–30 working days after passing the driving test. Dispatch via Speed Post or direct collection at RTO as per state policy. Renewal: 7–15 days. Source: parivahan.gov.in
sections.validity: Permanent DL validity (per parivahan.gov.in): If age at issue is less than 30 years: valid till the holder attains 40 years of age. After age 40: renewed for 10 years. Subsequently: renewed for 5 years each time. Transport/Commercial DL: valid for 5 years from date of issue. Learner Licence: valid for 6 months from date of issue. Learner Licence cannot be renewed — if expired, must apply for fresh LL. Source: parivahan.gov.in
applicationSteps: Step 1 — Apply for Learner Licence (LL) at sarathi.parivahan.gov.in → Select your State → Learner's Licence → Apply for LL
Step 2 — Fill application form online, upload documents, pay LL fee (₹200)
Step 3 — Book LL test slot at your nearest RTO through the Sarathi portal
Step 4 — Visit RTO on test date with: acknowledgement slip, original documents (Aadhaar, age proof, Form 1/1A, fee receipt)
Step 5 — Pass the computer-based LL test on traffic rules and road signs at RTO
Step 6 — Receive Learner Licence (valid for 6 months). Display L-plate on vehicle while practising
Step 7 — After minimum 30 days from LL issue, apply for Permanent DL at sarathi.parivahan.gov.in → New Driving Licence
Step 8 — Book driving test slot, pay DL fee (₹200)
Step 9 — Visit RTO on driving test date with: valid LL, original documents, a registered and insured vehicle of the class applied for
Step 10 — Pass the driving test conducted by RTO Motor Vehicle Inspector
Step 11 — DL dispatched to registered address via Speed Post (15–30 days) or collected at RTO as per state policy
Step 12 — Track application status at sarathi.parivahan.gov.in → Application Status

learnerLicence.overview: A Learner Licence (LL) is a temporary permit allowing practice of driving under supervision of a permanent DL holder. Mandatory before applying for permanent DL. Valid for 6 months. Valid throughout India. Source: parivahan.gov.in
learnerLicence.testFormat: Computer-based online test on traffic signs, rules, and road safety at the RTO. Must pass to receive LL. If failed or absent: pay retest fee and book fresh appointment at sarathi.parivahan.gov.in. Can retry after 60 days of last failed attempt (for fee payment). Source: parivahan.gov.in
learnerLicence.afterLL: Practice driving with an L-plate on vehicle. A permanent DL holder must be present in the vehicle. Wait at least 30 days after LL issue before applying for permanent DL. LL cannot be renewed — must get fresh LL if expired. Source: parivahan.gov.in

renewal.overview: DL must be renewed before expiry or within the grace period. Expired DL (more than 1 year): requires fresh driving test or LL issuance. Source: parivahan.gov.in
renewal.process: Apply online at sarathi.parivahan.gov.in → Services on Driver Licence → Renewal of DL. Upload medical fitness certificate (Form 1A mandatory for age 40+ and transport vehicles). Pay renewal fee ₹200. Visit RTO if biometrics needed.
renewal.lateRenewal: If DL expired for more than 1 year: additional late fee of ₹1,000 per year of delay beyond grace period. May need fresh driving test or LL. Source: parivahan.gov.in

sections.duplicateLicence: If DL is lost or damaged: Apply at sarathi.parivahan.gov.in → Services on Driving Licence → Duplicate DL. Submit: FIR copy for lost DL, damaged DL for damaged case, Aadhaar. Fee: ₹200. Processing: 7–15 days. Source: parivahan.gov.in
sections.internationalDrivingPermit: Apply at RTO with: valid Indian DL, Passport, Visa, Photographs. Fee: ₹1,000. Valid for 1 year. Not all countries accept IDP — verify with destination country. Source: parivahan.gov.in
commonMistakes: Applying for permanent DL before completing 30 days after LL issue — application will be rejected (Source: parivahan.gov.in)
Not carrying required original documents to RTO on test day
Vehicle used for driving test not having valid registration (RC) and insurance — test cannot be conducted
Selecting wrong vehicle class — you can only drive vehicles of the class endorsed on your DL
Not booking LL test slot online — must book at sarathi.parivahan.gov.in (Source: parivahan.gov.in)
LL expired before taking DL test — must get fresh LL (LL cannot be renewed per parivahan.gov.in)
Medical certificate (Form 1A) not obtained for age 40+ — required per parivahan.gov.in

edgeCases.title: Learner Licence expired — could not take DL test
edgeCases.problem: LL expired (6 months passed) before the applicant could appear for the driving test.
edgeCases.recommendedNextStep: As per parivahan.gov.in, LL cannot be renewed. Must apply for a fresh LL at sarathi.parivahan.gov.in, pass the LL test again, wait 30 days, then apply for DL.

edgeCases.title: Failed driving test
edgeCases.problem: Applicant failed the RTO driving test.
edgeCases.recommendedNextStep: Pay retest fee and book fresh appointment at sarathi.parivahan.gov.in → Fee Payments. Can retry as long as LL is still valid. Fee deposit allowed after 60 days of last failed attempt. Source: parivahan.gov.in

edgeCases.title: DL approved in portal but not received
edgeCases.problem: Application status shows DL approved but physical card not received.
edgeCases.recommendedNextStep: Track dispatch status at sarathi.parivahan.gov.in → Application Status. If still not received: contact your Zonal Office or Transport Department with original documents. Source: parivahan.gov.in

faqs.question: What is learner licence?
faqs.answer: A Learner Licence is a temporary permit valid for 6 months allowing you to practise driving under supervision of a permanent DL holder. You must display an L-plate on the vehicle. Mandatory before applying for permanent DL. Valid throughout India. Source: parivahan.gov.in

faqs.question: What is the minimum age for driving licence?
faqs.answer: 18 years for cars and geared motorcycles. 16 years for gearless scooters/mopeds up to 50cc (with parent consent). 20 years for transport/commercial vehicles. Source: Motor Vehicles Act / parivahan.gov.in

faqs.question: What documents are needed for driving licence?
faqs.answer: Aadhaar card, Form 1 (medical self-declaration), Form 1A (for age 40+), existing valid Learner Licence, photographs. For driving test: vehicle with valid RC and insurance. Source: parivahan.gov.in

faqs.question: How much is driving licence fee?
faqs.answer: Learner Licence: ₹200. Permanent DL: ₹200. Renewal: ₹200. International Driving Permit: ₹1,000. Duplicate DL: ₹200. Late renewal: ₹300 + ₹1,000 per year delay. Source: parivahan.gov.in

faqs.question: How to apply for driving licence?
faqs.answer: Apply for LL at sarathi.parivahan.gov.in. Pass LL test at RTO. Wait 30 days. Apply for permanent DL. Pass driving test at RTO. DL dispatched in 15–30 days. Source: parivahan.gov.in

faqs.question: How long is driving licence valid?
faqs.answer: If issued before age 30: valid till you turn 40. After 40: renewed for 10 years, then 5 years each time. Transport DL: 5 years. Learner Licence: 6 months. Source: parivahan.gov.in

faqs.question: Can learner licence be renewed?
faqs.answer: No. Learner Licence cannot be renewed. If expired, you must apply for a fresh Learner Licence at sarathi.parivahan.gov.in. Source: parivahan.gov.in

faqs.question: How to renew expired driving licence?
faqs.answer: Apply at sarathi.parivahan.gov.in → Services on Driver Licence → Renewal. Fee: ₹200. Medical certificate (Form 1A) required for age 40+. If expired more than 1 year: late fee ₹300 + ₹1,000/year, may need fresh driving test. Source: parivahan.gov.in

faqs.question: What if I failed driving test?
faqs.answer: Pay retest fee and book fresh appointment at sarathi.parivahan.gov.in. Can retry as long as LL is valid. Fee can be deposited after 60 days of last failed attempt. Source: parivahan.gov.in

faqs.question: How to get duplicate driving licence?
faqs.answer: Apply at sarathi.parivahan.gov.in → Services on Driving Licence → Duplicate DL. Submit FIR (for lost), Aadhaar. Fee: ₹200. Source: parivahan.gov.in

faqs.question: Is learner licence valid throughout India?
faqs.answer: Yes. Learner Licence is valid throughout India. Source: parivahan.gov.in

faqs.question: Do I need medical certificate for driving licence?
faqs.answer: Form 1 (self-declaration) is required for all applicants. Form 1A (certificate from registered doctor) is mandatory for applicants aged 40 and above, and for transport/commercial vehicle licences. Source: parivahan.gov.in
